perturbation.original_text: (2) You and your representatives agree that the Confidential Evaluation Material will be used solely for the purpose of evaluating the proposed transaction. You also agree that you and your officers, employees, financing sources, consultants, Affiliates, agents and advisors, herein collectively referred to as “ your representatives ,” will not disclose or permit the disclosure of any of the Confidential Evaluation Material now or hereafter received or obtained from the Company or its representatives to any third party or otherwise use or permit the use of the Confidential Evaluation Material in any way detrimental to the Company, except as required by court order or legal process, without the prior written consent of the Company, provided, however, that any such information may be disclosed to such of your representatives who need to know such information for the purpose of evaluating the proposed transaction and who are advised of this agreement and agree to keep such information confidential and to be bound by this agreement to the same extent as if they were parties hereto, it being understood that you shall be responsible for any breach of this agreement by your representatives; provided, however, that you shall not be responsible for any failure to comply with the terms of this agreement by any of your representatives that (i) is not one of your officers, employees or Affiliates and (ii) has signed an undertaking in favor of the Company and you to the effect of Annex A hereto.
perturbation.changed_text: (2) You and your representatives agree that the Confidential Evaluation Material will be used solely for the purpose of evaluating the proposed transaction. You also agree that you and your officers, employees, financing sources, consultants, Affiliates, agents and advisors, herein collectively referred to as “ your representatives ,” will not disclose or permit the disclosure of any of the Confidential Evaluation Material now or hereafter received or obtained from the Company or its representatives to any third party or otherwise use or permit the use of the Confidential Evaluation Material in any way detrimental to the Company, except as required by court order or legal process, without the prior written consent of the Company. However, you are not responsible for any breach of this agreement by your representatives, regardless of whether they have signed an undertaking or are officers, employees, or affiliates.
perturbation.explanation: This change contradicts the original clause regarding the responsibility of the signing party (Silver Lake) for breaches of the agreement by their representatives. Originally, Silver Lake was responsible for breaches by all representatives except those who signed the undertaking in Annex A and were not officers, employees or Affiliates. The modified version completely removes Silver Lake's responsibility, creating an in-text contradiction with the original agreement where representatives are still bound by the agreement as noted in this same section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (2) You and your representatives agree that the Confidential Evaluation Material will be used solely for the purpose of evaluating the proposed transaction. You also agree that you and your officers, employees, financing sources, consultants, Affiliates, agents and advisors, herein collectively referred to as “ your representatives ,” will not disclose or permit the disclosure of any of the Confidential Evaluation Material now or hereafter received or obtained from the Company or its representatives to any third party or otherwise use or permit the use of the Confidential Evaluation Material in any way detrimental to the Company, except as required by court order or legal process, without the prior written consent of the Company. However, you are not responsible for any breach of this agreement by your representatives, regardless of whether they have signed an undertaking or are officers, employees, or affiliates.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the responsibility of Silver Lake for breaches of the agreement by its representatives, regardless of whether they have signed an undertaking or are officers, employees, or affiliates. This directly contradicts the earlier part of the same section (provided as the 'Contradicted Text') which states that representatives are bound by the agreement. The original text held Silver Lake responsible for the actions of its representatives under certain conditions, while the changed text absolves them of all responsibility, creating a clear and direct conflict within the same section of the agreement.

perturbation.original_text: (6) At any time upon the Company’s request, all Confidential Evaluation Material (and all copies, summaries, and notes of the contents or parts thereof) shall be returned or, if you so choose, destroyed and not retained by you or your representatives in any form or for any reason and written certification to that effect will be sent by you to the Company within 30 days of such request; provided, however, that you and your representatives shall not be obligated to return or destroy the Confidential Evaluation Material if, and to the extent, otherwise required by any applicable law, regulation, policy or procedures relating to the retention, back-up storage or automatic archiving of files and data. Any Confidential Evaluation Material that is retained by you or your representatives for the sole purpose of compliance with such law, regulation, policy or procedures shall not be used for any purpose other than to evidence compliance therewith and such retained material and information shall continue to be subject to the confidentiality obligations set forth in this letter agreement, which obligations shall survive any termination hereof. All the Confidential Evaluation Material will be and remains the property of the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: (6) At any time upon the Company’s request, all Confidential Evaluation Material (and all copies, summaries, and notes of the contents or parts thereof) shall be returned or, if you so choose, destroyed and not retained by you or your representatives in any form or for any reason and written certification to that effect will be sent by you to the Company within 30 days of such request; provided, however, that you and your representatives shall not be obligated to return or destroy the Confidential Evaluation Material if, and to the extent, otherwise required by any applicable law, regulation, policy or procedures relating to the retention, back-up storage or automatic archiving of files and data. Any Confidential Evaluation Material that is retained by you or your representatives can be used for purposes other than compliance with such law, regulation, policy or procedures. All the Confidential Evaluation Material will be and remains the property of the Company.
perturbation.explanation: The modified version allows the retained Confidential Evaluation Material to be used for purposes other than compliance, contradicting the original agreement's strict limitation. This creates an in-text contradiction as the original agreement states the confidential obligation will survive any termination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (6) At any time upon the Company’s request, all Confidential Evaluation Material (and all copies, summaries, and notes of the contents or parts thereof) shall be returned or, if you so choose, destroyed and not retained by you or your representatives in any form or for any reason and written certification to that effect will be sent by you to the Company within 30 days of such request; provided, however, that you and your representatives shall not be obligated to return or destroy the Confidential Evaluation Material if, and to the extent, otherwise required by any applicable law, regulation, policy or procedures relating to the retention, back-up storage or automatic archiving of files and data. Any Confidential Evaluation Material that is retained by you or your representatives can be used for purposes other than compliance with such law, regulation, policy or procedures. All the Confidential Evaluation Material will be and remains the property of the Company.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the conditions under which Confidential Evaluation Material can be used if retained. The original text implied use only for compliance, but the changed text allows for other purposes. This contradicts the requirement that retained material remains subject to the original confidentiality obligations, creating an in-text contradiction because it undermines the surviving confidentiality obligations as stated in the Perturbation Explanation, and directly impacts the contradicted text (which is the changed text itself). Therefore, according to Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction definition, and the explanations provided there is a clear in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: (10) You agree that for a period of one year from the date of this letter agreement, neither you nor your representatives will, without the prior written consent of the Company, directly or indirectly: (A) acquire, offer to acquire, or agree to acquire, directly or indirectly, by purchase or otherwise, any securities or direct or indirect rights to acquire any securities of the Company or any subsidiary thereof, or of any successor to or person in control of the Company, or any assets of the Company or any division thereof or of any such successor or controlling person; provided, however, that nothing herein shall prohibit the purchase of securities in the ordinary course of any of your representatives’ business that does not in any event result in aggregate ownership by such representative of more than 2% of the outstanding amount of any class of securities of the Company; (B) seek or propose to influence or control the management or policies of the Company, make or in any way participate, directly or indirectly, in any “solicitation” of “proxies” (as such terms are used in the rules of the Securities and Exchange Commission) to vote any voting securities of the Company or any subsidiary thereof, or seek to advise or influence any person or entity with respect to the voting of any voting securities of the Company or any subsidiary thereof; (C) make any public announcement with respect to, or submit a proposal for or offer of (with or without conditions), any merger, recapitalization, reorganization, business combination or other extraordinary transaction involving the Company or any subsidiary thereof or any of their securities or assets; or (D) enter into any discussions, negotiations, arrangements or understandings with any third party with respect to any of the foregoing, or otherwise form, join or in any way engage in discussions relating to the formation of, or participate in, a “group” within the meaning of Section 13(d)(3) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, in connection with any of the foregoing.
perturbation.changed_text: (10) You agree that for a period of one year from the date of this letter agreement, neither you nor your representatives will, without the prior written consent of the Company, directly or indirectly: (A) acquire, offer to acquire, or agree to acquire, directly or indirectly, by purchase or otherwise, any securities or direct or indirect rights to acquire any securities of the Company or any subsidiary thereof, or of any successor to or person in control of the Company, or any assets of the Company or any division thereof or of any such successor or controlling person; provided, however, that nothing herein shall prohibit the purchase of securities in the ordinary course of any of your representatives’ business, even if such purchase results in aggregate ownership by such representative of more than 2% of the outstanding amount of any class of securities of the Company; (B) seek or propose to influence or control the management or policies of the Company, make or in any way participate, directly or indirectly, in any “solicitation” of “proxies” (as such terms are used in the rules of the Securities and Exchange Commission) to vote any voting securities of the Company or any subsidiary thereof, or seek to advise or influence any person or entity with respect to the voting of any voting securities of the Company or any subsidiary thereof; (C) make any public announcement with respect to, or submit a proposal for or offer of (with or without conditions), any merger, recapitalization, reorganization, business combination or other extraordinary transaction involving the Company or any subsidiary thereof or any of their securities or assets; or (D) enter into any discussions, negotiations, arrangements or understandings with any third party with respect to any of the foregoing, or otherwise form, join or in any way engage in discussions relating to the formation of, or participate in, a “group” within the meaning of Section 13(d)(3) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, in connection with any of the foregoing.
perturbation.explanation: By modifying the exception in part (A) of paragraph 10, the agreement now allows representatives to purchase securities exceeding the 2% limit, directly contradicting the original restriction set forth in that paragraph. This creates an in-text contradiction as the original agreement stated the restrictions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (10) You agree that for a period of one year from the date of this letter agreement, neither you nor your representatives will, without the prior written consent of the Company, directly or indirectly: (A) acquire, offer to acquire, or agree to acquire, directly or indirectly, by purchase or otherwise, any securities or direct or indirect rights to acquire any securities of the Company or any subsidiary thereof, or of any successor to or person in control of the Company, or any assets of the Company or any division thereof or of any such successor or controlling person; provided, however, that nothing herein shall prohibit the purchase of securities in the ordinary course of any of your representatives’ business, even if such purchase results in aggregate ownership by such representative of more than 2% of the outstanding amount of any class of securities of the Company; (B) seek or propose to influence or control the management or policies of the Company, make or in any way participate, directly or indirectly, in any “solicitation” of “proxies” (as such terms are used in the rules of the Securities and Exchange Commission) to vote any voting securities of the Company or any subsidiary thereof, or seek to advise or influence any person or entity with respect to the voting of any voting securities of the Company or any subsidiary thereof; (C) make any public announcement with respect to, or submit a proposal for or offer of (with or without conditions), any merger, recapitalization, reorganization, business combination or other extraordinary transaction involving the Company or any subsidiary thereof or any of their securities or assets; or (D) enter into any discussions, negotiations, arrangements or understandings with any third party with respect to any of the foregoing, or otherwise form, join or in any way engage in discussions relating to the formation of, or participate in, a “group” within the meaning of Section 13(d)(3) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, in connection with any of the foregoing.
perturbation.justification: The original text in paragraph 10(A) prohibited representatives from acquiring securities exceeding 2% of the outstanding amount. The changed text modifies the exception, allowing representatives to purchase securities even if it results in ownership exceeding 2%. This directly contradicts the initial restriction set forth in the original text of paragraph 10(A), creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: (11) The provisions of this agreement relating to confidentiality shall terminate two years from the date hereof. The invalidity or enforceability of any provision of this agreement shall not affect the validity or enforceability of any other provision.
perturbation.changed_text: (11) The provisions of this agreement relating to confidentiality shall terminate immediately from the date hereof. The invalidity or enforceability of any provision of this agreement shall not affect the validity or enforceability of any other provision.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the confidentiality termination period to 'immediately' creates an immediate contradiction with all clauses that depend on the confidentiality of the material, namely paragraph (2) where it agrees that the Confidential Evaluation Material will not disclose to any third party.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (2) You and your representatives agree that the Confidential Evaluation Material will be used solely for the purpose of evaluating the proposed transaction. You also agree that you and your officers, employees, financing sources, consultants, Affiliates, agents and advisors, herein collectively referred to as “ your representatives ,” will not disclose or permit the disclosure of any of the Confidential Evaluation Material now or hereafter received or obtained from the Company or its representatives to any third party or otherwise use or permit the use of the Confidential Evaluation Material in any way detrimental to the Company, except as required by court order or legal process, without the prior written consent of the Company. However, you are not responsible for any breach of this agreement by your representatives, regardless of whether they have signed an undertaking or are officers, employees, or affiliates.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified a two-year termination for confidentiality, while the changed text mandates immediate termination. Paragraph (2) depends on ongoing confidentiality for the Confidential Evaluation Material to not be disclosed to third parties. The change makes it impossible to enforce the confidentiality agreement in (2) as confidentiality terminates immediately per the modified (11), which creates a direct conflict and thus a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: (13) “You” used alone shall mean Silver Lake Management Company, L.L.C. and its officers, employees and Affiliates.
perturbation.changed_text: (13) “You” used alone shall mean Silver Lake Management Company, L.L.C.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'its officers, employees and Affiliates' from the definition of "You", this directly contradicts all sections where the obligations of "You" extend to its representatives. This creates ambiguity and potential in-text contradiction regarding the scope of responsibility for Silver Lake, its officers, and affiliates, specifically with the terms to Confidential Evaluation Material.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (2) You and your representatives agree that the Confidential Evaluation Material will be used solely for the purpose of evaluating the proposed transaction. You also agree that you and your officers, employees, financing sources, consultants, Affiliates, agents and advisors, herein collectively referred to as “ your representatives ,” will not disclose or permit the disclosure of any of the Confidential Evaluation Material now or hereafter received or obtained from the Company or its representatives to any third party or otherwise use or permit the use of the Confidential Evaluation Material in any way detrimental to the Company, except as required by court order or legal process, without the prior written consent of the Company. However, you are not responsible for any breach of this agreement by your representatives, regardless of whether they have signed an undertaking or are officers, employees, or affiliates.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes 'its officers, employees and Affiliates' from the definition of 'You'. The contradicted text states 'You and your representatives agree that the Confidential Evaluation Material will be used solely for the purpose of evaluating the proposed transaction' and 'You also agree that you and your officers, employees, financing sources, consultants, Affiliates, agents and advisors, herein collectively referred to as “ your representatives ,” will not disclose or permit the disclosure of any of the Confidential Evaluation Material'. By narrowing the definition of 'You', the changed text creates a direct conflict and ambiguity with the contradicted text regarding the responsibilities and liabilities of Silver Lake's representatives concerning the Confidential Evaluation Material. The contradiction exists because the responsibilities of 'You' now do not extend to its officers, employees, and Affiliates. The original text included these parties in the definition of 'You'.

perturbation.original_text: (4) Except as may be required by court order or legal process, neither party hereto nor any of its representatives will disclose to any other person or entity: (a) the fact that information regarding the Company is being or has been furnished to you; (b) the fact that discussions or negotiations regarding any transaction are or have been taking place between representatives of you and the Company, or any information regarding the status or terms of any such discussions or negotiations between the Company and you or the identity of the parties thereto; (c) the fact that the Company is or has been considering the possibility of entering into a transaction with you of the nature discussed by you and the Company. In addition, neither you nor your representatives will disclose to any other person or entity that the Company has been considering the possibility of entering into a transaction of the nature discussed by you and the Company, with any third party. Either party hereto shall be permitted to disclose any fact or information covered by this paragraph 4 to the same extent, and under the same conditions, as you are permitted to disclose Confidential Evaluation Material pursuant to paragraphs 2 and 3 hereof. Nothing in this paragraph (4) shall be construed to prevent or limit the Company’s or its representatives’ right to disclose, discuss, negotiate or enter into a transaction of the nature discussed by you and the Company with any other person or entity or provide information regarding the Company to any other person or entity.
perturbation.changed_text: (4) Except as may be required by court order or legal process, neither party hereto nor any of its representatives will disclose to any other person or entity: (a) the fact that information regarding the Company is being or has been furnished to you; (b) the fact that discussions or negotiations regarding any transaction are or have been taking place between representatives of you and the Company, or any information regarding the status or terms of any such discussions or negotiations between the Company and you or the identity of the parties thereto; (c) the fact that the Company is or has been considering the possibility of entering into a transaction with you of the nature discussed by you and the Company. In addition, neither you nor your representatives will disclose to any other person or entity that the Company has been considering the possibility of entering into a transaction of the nature discussed by you and the Company, with any third party. Either party hereto shall be permitted to disclose any fact or information covered by this paragraph 4 without any conditions, as you are permitted to disclose Confidential Evaluation Material pursuant to paragraphs 2 and 3 hereof. Nothing in this paragraph (4) shall be construed to prevent or limit the Company’s or its representatives’ right to disclose, discuss, negotiate or enter into a transaction of the nature discussed by you and the Company with any other person or entity or provide information regarding the Company to any other person or entity.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the phrase 'to the same extent, and under the same conditions' to 'without any conditions', it creates an in-text contradiction where the restrictions on the release of Confidential Evaluation Material are nullified, even when paragraphs 2 and 3 state there are conditions when doing so, for instance, court orders or legal process.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (4) Except as may be required by court order or legal process, neither party hereto nor any of its representatives will disclose to any other person or entity: (a) the fact that information regarding the Company is being or has been furnished to you; (b) the fact that discussions or negotiations regarding any transaction are or have been taking place between representatives of you and the Company, or any information regarding the status or terms of any such discussions or negotiations between the Company and you or the identity of the parties thereto; (c) the fact that the Company is or has been considering the possibility of entering into a transaction with you of the nature discussed by you and the Company. In addition, neither you nor your representatives will disclose to any other person or entity that the Company has been considering the possibility of entering into a transaction of the nature discussed by you and the Company, with any third party. Either party hereto shall be permitted to disclose any fact or information covered by this paragraph 4 without any conditions, as you are permitted to disclose Confidential Evaluation Material pursuant to paragraphs 2 and 3 hereof. Nothing in this paragraph (4) shall be construed to prevent or limit the Company’s or its representatives’ right to disclose, discuss, negotiate or enter into a transaction of the nature discussed by you and the Company with any other person or entity or provide information regarding the Company to any other person or entity.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies paragraph 4 by stating that either party can disclose information covered by that paragraph 'without any conditions,' while still referencing paragraphs 2 and 3, which DO contain conditions regarding the disclosure of Confidential Evaluation Material (such as court orders or legal processes). This creates a direct conflict because the changed text implies unconditional disclosure, while paragraphs 2 and 3 impose conditions. This is a strong contradiction because it directly affects the enforceability of the confidentiality provisions.
